snapshots_overview.title: Saved Setups — Your Trusted Recall List
snapshots_overview.content:
Saved Setups let you capture your current patch and bring it back later. Think of them as trusted recall points for your audio configuration.

Perfect for:
• Switching between different setups (work, music, gaming)
• Experimenting without fear
• Quickly getting back to a known-good patch
snapshots_overview.tip: Press S to jump to Saved Setups in the left navigation. Save before big changes, restore when you want to get back on track.

what_gets_saved.title: What Gets Saved?
what_gets_saved.content:
A saved setup captures:

🔗 Connections - Which nodes are wired to which
🔊 Volumes - All volume levels and mute states
📍 Positions - Where nodes are placed on the graph

Basically, everything you'd need to recreate your exact setup!
what_gets_saved.tip: Saved setups are stored locally - they persist even after closing Pipeflow.

creating_snapshots.title: Saving a Setup
creating_snapshots.content:
To save your current setup:

1. Open Saved Setups (S)
2. Click 'Save Setup'
3. Give it a descriptive name

The setup is saved immediately with the current timestamp.
creating_snapshots.tip: Use descriptive names like 'Streaming Setup v2' or 'Recording Session 2024-01' - future you will thank you!

restoring_snapshots.title: Restoring a Saved Setup
restoring_snapshots.content:
To restore a saved setup:

1. Open Saved Setups (S)
2. Find the setup you want
3. Click 'Restore'

Pipeflow will recreate the connections and settings as best it can.

Note: If some nodes no longer exist (apps closed, devices unplugged), those connections will be skipped.
restoring_snapshots.tip: Restore only affects nodes that exist - it won't launch applications for you.

partial_restore.title: Smart Matching
partial_restore.content:
When you restore, Pipeflow intelligently matches nodes:

• It looks for nodes with the same name and type
• If an exact match isn't found, it tries similar matches
• Unmatched connections are skipped (no errors!)

This means saved setups still work even when your system changes a bit.
partial_restore.tip: Node IDs change when apps restart - that's why Pipeflow matches by name and type instead.

snapshot_management.title: Managing Saved Setups
snapshot_management.content:
In Saved Setups you can:

• See all saved setups with timestamps
• Preview what each setup contains (connections, volumes)
• Restore any setup
• Delete setups you no longer need

Saved setups don't take much space, so keep as many as you like!
snapshot_management.tip: You can create multiple saved setups for the same basic patch - one for each variation you may want later.
